lesson.title: PRODUCTION
topics: Production is the process by which goods and services are created to satisfy human wants and
needs. It has been a basis of economic development in many countries. While production has led
to unprecedented economic growth, improved living standards, and technological advancements,
it has also brought about severe environmental consequences that continue to affect our country
activities: environment during the extraction, processing and transportation of raw materials as well as the
The topic will look at the Stages of Production, Specialisation, Factors of Production and
teacher is expected to facilitate a learning process that allows learners to explore and discover
concepts under this topic on their own.
resources: These are either above or underground.
the forest, animals in the wild and fertile soil and good climate.
Forestry: Harvesting of timber and other forestry products in the forest.
assessment: Explain production in relation to the activities practiced in your community
Learners to find out about the things in life that they can do without (luxuries) and those
they cannot do without (necessities) in order to survive
